attestation_note: Attested by Yu-Chen Huang. Every claim here survived my review. Corrected errors are listed below; the percentage metrics in work highlights are my own rough estimates (see metrics_disclosure), not formal measurements.
corrected_errors: 1) PROJECT OWNERSHIP: the E3 Database System was written in solo voice ('Built', 'Developed', 'Applied') but it was actually a 5-person group project for Northeastern's DAMG6210 (repo: github.com/Fxhj0501/DAMG6210). Reframed as team work; my part was schema design and stored procedures.

2) PROJECT ORIGINALITY: the IPL Data Analysis project was presented like original engineering, but it was a guided learning project built by following a YouTube tutorial (per the repo README). Relabeled as a learning/tutorial project.
3) DATE TYPO: the E3 project start read 'Step 2024' on the resume; corrected to September 2024 (2024-09).

4) SKILLS PROVENANCE: R, PostgreSQL, MySQL, Docker, and CI/CD were listed as if job-proven; they actually come from coursework (e.g. Network Structures and Cloud Computing, Data Management and Database Design). Evidence relabeled to 'coursework'.

5) METRICS: the percentages in my work highlights (95%, 99.9% SLA, 40%, 60%, 50%, 25%) are rough estimates I made, not measured figures. Disclosed in metrics_disclosure so I never present them as precise.
metrics_disclosure: The percentage figures in work[].highlights are my own approximate estimates of impact, not formally measured numbers. I can explain my reasoning behind each, but they should be read as 'approximately', not as audited metrics.

work.title: Data Engineer Intern
highlights: Developed end-to-end reporting workflow using Python, Airflow, and HTML/CSS to distribute ML-evaluated sentiment and performance summaries with Tableau analytics links, enhancing weekly CSAT transparency across 50+ leaders
Designed AD group sync pipeline using Python and FastAPI to parse HR data and update distribution lists automatically, cutting manual maintenance time by 95% and reducing IT workload

Built near real-time KPI pipeline using Kafka, Spark Structured Streaming, and Airflow to ingest and transform service event data, enabling sub-5-minute latency reporting across 20+ downstream applications

Refactored and productionized ETL pipelines using Python, Airflow, and Spark, implementing data validation, logging, and automated retry mechanisms; reduced data discrepancies from 8% to 1% and improved pipeline reliability to 99.9% SLA

work.title: Data Engineer Intern
highlights: Built scalable ETL pipelines using SQL and Oracle to process over million+ financial records daily for batch reporting systems
Optimized Oracle schemas using partitioning and indexing for scalable batch ingestion, cutting storage overhead by 40%
Orchestrated ETL workflows in Trinity by designing modular DAGs, ensuring timely execution of 100% of daily batch jobs
Automated data validation workflows with Python and SQL, ensuring 100% data integrity and compliance with banking standards

work.title: Data Analyst, Media
highlights: Developed ETL pipelines in Python and SQL to process large-scale survey data, improving data ingestion efficiency by 50%
Engineered survey data models and transformation scripts, enabling accurate forecasting using Statsmodels and Pandas
Implemented data partitioning strategies in S3 and optimized storage formats, resulting in 60% reduction in data processing costs
Integrated Redshift warehouse data into Tableau using custom SQL, enabling real-time tracking across 10+ media campaigns

projects.name: Electronic Entertainment Expo Database System
projects.summary: 5-person team project. The team built a 10+ table relational database in MS SQL Server (100K+ records with indexing, triggers, stored procedures), applied 3NF normalization to reduce storage by ~25%, and developed a Java GUI for CRUD operations with Power BI visualizations.

projects.name: IPL Data Analysis on Databricks
projects.type: guided learning project (followed a YouTube tutorial)
projects.summary: Self-study project built by following a YouTube tutorial: an Apache Spark ETL pipeline on AWS S3 using PySpark for 100K+ IPL records, advanced SQL (joins, subqueries, aggregations, window functions), and 5+ visualizations with Matplotlib and Seaborn.
